items.title: CSCwf31642 - Intersight Managed Mode: Windows OS (2019/2022) install fails on IMM (Cisco  UCS C220 M7 and C240 M7) servers with iSCSI targets
items.text: Microsoft Windows 2019 and Windows 2022 OS install fails on Intersight Managed Mode Cisco UCS C220 M7 and UCS C240 M7 servers. This issue is specifically observed on iSCSI targets.

items.title: CSCvv56927 - Intersight Managed Mode: connect adapter command is not working for Cisco UCS X-Series servers
items.text: M6 Blade Adapter Interface does not get created. This results in adapter command failure.

items.title: CSCwe88424 - HyperFlex clusters with the Base license show 'None'
items.text: Intersight displays "None" for the HyperFlex Cluster Intersight license, even though all servers in the cluster are assigned to a license tier.

items.title: CSCwe72708 - Intersight Managed Mode: Adapter Compliance is not getting listed under HCL tab on ESXi 7.0u1
items.text: For VIC 1467 and Intel x710T2LG adapters, HCL validation tab> Adapter Compliance>Software Status shows "Incompatible component firmware and driver” with a warning message “Firmware version not listed in HCL".

items.title: CSCwe40513 - Intersight Managed Mode: HCL compliance fails to list HCL compliance for Cisco UCS C220/240 M7 servers
items.text: In Intersight, for Cisco UCS C220/240 M7 servers, HCL compliance is not getting listed.

items.title: CSCwe29749/CSCwe50899 - Intersight Managed Mode: Cisco UCS X210c M7 Compute Node fails to be listed under HCL Validations tab
items.text: In Intersight, Cisco UCS X210c M7 Compute Node hardware compliance is not getting listed under HCL Validations tab.

items.title: CSCwc96598  - Intersight Managed Mode: Bundle version is not seen for already claimed Fabric Interconnects
items.text: Bundle version is not seen for already claimed Fabric Interconnects. The bundle version comes up once the upgrade firmware is successful.

items.title: CSCvn57742  - Intersight Managed Mode: The usage of sub-policies is not displayed in the policy view page
items.text: The usage of sub-policies like Ethernet adapter policy, Ethernet network policy, Ethernet QoS policy, FC QoS policy, FC Network policy, and FC adapter policy, is not displayed in the policy view page.

items.title: CSCwa09515  - Infrastructure: Adapters, NIC and HBA interfaces count in server view might not be accurate for all hardware configurations
items.text: In the current implementation, the count for these interfaces is derived from CIMC ComputeRackUnit MO. The NIC and HBA interfaces count provides information only for VIC cards and does not include interfaces of non-Cisco adapters (NIC and FC HBA PCI cards).

items.title: CSCwc80899 - HyperFlex: hx_post_install fails on expanded NIC-based cluster
items.text: hx_post_install fails on expanded NIC-based custer with vMotion already configured. The vMotion switch cannot be found.

items.title: CSCwb20925  - Intersight Managed Mode: Server Inventory fails to display the latest Server Certificate
items.text: In Cisco UCS B-Series servers, the Server Inventory Page does not display the latest Server Certificate due to delayed Unified Endpoint Management (UEM) events.

items.title: CSCwb90747 - Intersight Cloud Orchestrator: HSM Enabled field goes to disabled state even after enabling in the UI
items.text: For an Intersight Account with Intersight Workload Optimizer (IWO) license, the Hardware Support Manager (HSM) will go back to disabled state even after enabling HSM in the UI. However, the HSM functionality works as expected.

items.title: CSCwb45235 - Intersight Cloud Orchestrator: Support for variables mapping to and from serial loop tasks
items.text: Variable support is added for Serial Loop.

items.title: CSCwb30421 - Intersight Cloud Orchestrator: Workflow execution terminates if input array size is less than the loop count or condition
items.text: Workflow execution continues when input array size is less than the loop count or condition

items.title: CSCvz22743  - Infrastructure: Download of firmware upgrade image fails when the proxy is not set in CIMC for M6 Standalone Rack Servers
items.text: During the workflow of direct upgrade of rack servers, the download of the firmware upgrade image fails when the proxy is not set in CIMC.

items.title: CSCvz44489 - Intersight Managed Mode: The Not Deployed Changes message is not displayed when the UUID property is modified.
items.text: When the UUID property is modified for a previously deployed Server Profile, the server profile status shows OK instead of Not Deployed Changes

items.title: CSCwa44246 - Intersight Managed Mode: Out-of-Band IP address on an Intersight Managed Mode server becomes unreachable after Fabric Interconnect reboot
items.text: The Out-of-Band IP address configured on an Intersight Managed Mode server using the IMC Access policy becomes unreachable after Fabric Interconnect reboot.

items.title: CSCvz25126 - Intersight Managed Mode: The power consumption reading of UCSX-210C-M6 server is lower than the actual power that the server consumes.
items.text: When the UCSX-210C-M6 server is powered on, the power consumption reading is lower than the actual power that the server consumes. The difference amount is -7% to -8% of the actual power.

items.title: CSCvz36957 - Intersight Managed Mode: The server UUID defined during server profile deployment is not displayed on the Server Inventory page for Cisco UCS C-series servers.
items.text: When the server profile deployment is complete, the latest UUID is not displayed on the Server Inventory page, even after the server restart. This issue is specifically observed on Cisco UCS C-series servers.

items.title: CSCvz08702 - Intersight Managed Mode: NVMe controllers are not displayed on the HCL Validation page
items.text: The Non-Volatile Memory Express (NVMe) controllers are not displayed in HCL Validation page. However, the controllers are displayed in the Server Inventory page. This will result in incorrect HCL evaluation.

items.title: CSCvv50863 – Intersight Managed Mode: ISO mapping fails when port numbers are explicitly mentioned in NI-HUU or NI-SCU config files
items.text: Upgrade from a custom port is not supported for servers running software version earlier than Cisco IMC 4.0(4m).

items.title: CSCvz84786 - Infrastructure: On the Software Downloads page, the tabs are not clickable for the existing users
items.text: The existing users are not able to click the tabs present on the Software Downloads page. New users do not face this issue.

items.title: CSCvz25321 - Intersight Managed Mode: Python OS Discovery Tool (ODT) fails on UCSX 210C M6 servers for Linux OS
items.text: Python ODT is currently not supported on UCSX 210C M6 servers for the Linux OS that includes Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL), SUSE Linux Enterprise Server(SLES), Ubuntu Sever, CentOS, and Oracle Linux. This will result in ODT script failure.

items.title: CSCvz72536 - Infrastructure: Organization edit operation is not working as expected
items.text: While editing the Organization name results in removal of the Resource Groups and editing the Organization name along with unselecting/selecting the Resource Group, results in the Organization edit page becoming unresponsive.

items.title: CSCvy00846 - Intersight Managed Mode: The user configuration on blades may be lost when there is a power outage to a rack hosting the Fabric Interconnect and Chassis
items.text: When there is a power outage to a rack, all user configuration on the blade servers may be lost.

items.title: CSCvz48955 - Intersight Virtual Appliance: The APIDocs for Storage Policy is not in sync with the UI and API
items.text: You can now fetch and modify Storage policies using APIDocs URI

items.title: CSCvx46827 - HyperFlex: HX Upgrade with an update to the latest ESXi patch fails if server firmware is already at the desired version
items.text: When you perform a combined HX upgrade with an update to the latest ESXi patch, the update node sub-workflow fails, if the server firmware is already at the desired version.

items.title: CSCvu88769 - Infrastructure: The embedded installation mode fails when using file server with domain name
items.text: The embedded installation mode fails when using file server with domain name.

items.title: CSCvv61398 - Infrastructure: Target claim fails if the password for the admin login includes any of the XML reserved
items.text: The issue of target claim failing if the password for the admin login includes any of the XML reserved characters (&, <, >, “, ‘) has been resolved.

items.title: CSCvz46580 - Intersight Managed Mode: vKVM SSO login might show Login denied after the system time is changed on Cisco IMC
items.text: vKVM SSO login might show Login denied after the system time is changed on the Cisco IMC version between 4.1(3a) and 4.1(3d).

items.title: CSCvz14268 – Intersight Managed Mode: For the cluster Fabric Interconnect, redeployment of the server profile fails on the rack server
items.text: Redeployment of the server profile on the rack server fails with the error that the Domain Profile is not associated with the Fabric Interconnects to which the server is attached. This error appears even though the Domain Profile was deployed on the Fabric Interconnects

items.title: CSCvy77588 – Intersight Managed Mode: M6 servers in IMM do not show the local disks during server discovery
items.text: The M6 servers in IMM do not show the local disks on server discovery after decommissioning/recommissioning.

items.title: CSCvz22743 - Intersight Managed Mode: Direct upgrade fails for M6 server when the proxy is not set in the Device Connector
items.text: Direct upgrade of M6 standalone servers fails if the proxy is not set on the device connector.

items.title: CSCvz14208 – Intersight Managed Mode: The Replace option is not available in the Detailed View page of the Fabric Interconnect that is to be replaced
items.text: Replace Fabric Interconnect and Replace UCS Domain options are added to the Actions menu in the Fabric Interconnect Detailed View page.

items.title: CSCvy65585 - Intersight Managed Mode: Physical disk and Virtual Drives states are not updated in Server inventory report
items.text: For M6 standalone servers, the state of the physical drives and virtual disks is not updated even after the storage policy is deployed. The virtual drive creation and set state actions are carried out, and the drive state alone is not updated in Server inventory page. This behavior is observed with firmware 4.2(1a). The fix is expected in 4.2(1b).

items.title: CSCvy50227 – Intersight Managed Mode: Upgrade Impact Error is observed when EFT image is selected for an upgrade
items.text: The selected image appears invalid when the user tries an upgrade using an EFT firmware bundle.

items.title: CSCvy21552 - Intersight Managed Mode: BIOS POST is pending during upgrade of Cisco UCS B200 M5 Blade Server for more than 12 minutes
items.text: The upgrade of Cisco UCS B200 M5 Blade Server is failed with timeout error as the BIOS POST was in pending status for more than 12 minutes. This issue is resolved in the infrastructure B and C bundles 4.1(3e).

items.title: CSCvy31340 - Security: Migration of One Identity to the identity provider Okta is complete
items.text: Multi-Factor Authentication now works for all Cisco domain users.

items.title: CSCvv35790 - Intersight Managed Mode: Tunneled vKVM fails when using server firmware version 4.1(2a)
items.text: Tunneled vKVM fails when using server firmware version 4.1(2a).

items.title: CSCvx43924 – Intersight Managed Mode: The discovery workflow of rack server gets into a loop and fails
items.text: When the firmware 4.1(3a) or 4.1(3b), is running on the endpoint, the discovery workflow of rack server may get into a loop and fails.

items.title: CSCvy35774 - Intersight Managed Mode: Launch of Tunneled vKVM fails with specific hostname format
items.text: Tunneled vKVM fails to launch with hostnames in the format of intersight.com or {account_moid}.intersight.com.

items.title: CSCvx39905 – Intersight Managed Mode: All the assigned policies may not sync when template configured with all the supported policies is attached to a Server Profile
items.text: When an imported standalone server profile is attached to a standalone server template configured with a completely different set of policies, then some of the server template policies may not sync to the server profile.

items.title: CSCvt20882 – Intersight Managed Mode: Chrome browser throws an error and does not let the user to proceed and launch KVM
items.text: This failure is due to stronger security restrictions in the browser for a self-signed server certificate. Trying another browser that allows such exceptions, such as Firefox, can solve the issue.

items.title: CSCvy08053 – Intersight Managed Mode: The false HDD missing alarms are displayed on servers
items.text: Though the HDD is not removed, the EquipmentFruMissing alarm is displayed.

items.title: CSCvx16605 - Infrastructure: The health status of a server or Fabric Interconnect is updated in the Cisco Intersight user interface, whereas the health status does not synchronize to the device console
items.text: The health status of a server or Fabric Interconnect is updated in the Cisco Intersight user interface, whereas the health status does not synchronize to the device console.

items.title: CSCvx58446 - UCSM Managed Mode: The UMM server upgrade workflow fails at the firmware image download stage
items.text: The UMM server upgrade workflow fails at the firmware image download stage.

items.title: CSCvx01686 - Infrastructure: State cannot be set for the physical drives on M.2 controller
items.text: Set State action of MSTOR-RAID/M.2 controller on physical disk tab is disabled.

items.title: CSCwa77606 - Kubernetes: Error while deleting worker node groups
items.text: An error occurs when you delete a Worker Node Group from the Worker Node Pools Configuration screen.

items.title: CSCvz66973 - Kubernetes: Unable to change a VM configuration
items.text: VM resizing is not supported.

items.title: CSCvy59071 - Kubernetes: Missing privileges for the Kubernetes Operator role
items.text: A user with the Kubernetes Operator role is not able to download the KubeConfig and deploy add-ons to an existing clusters.

items.title: CSCwa69598 - Kubernetes: Hyperlinks to the optional add-ons are disabled
items.text: Hyperlinks to the optional add-ons such as Grafana, Kubernetes Dashboard, and SMM Dashboard, are disabled in the Operate tab of the Cluster Details page.

items.title: CSCwa51286 - Kubernetes: Prometheus crashes repeatedly when Persistent Volume exceeds the configured storage capacity
items.text: Prometheus crashes repeatedly when you use Persistent Volume with the default size of 20 GB.

items.title: CSCwa25562 - Kubernetes: Add-on installation fails after an incomplete uninstall
items.text: If an add-on is not completely uninstalled, a subsequent add-on installation will fail. Even though the ‘helm -n iks ls` list command does not list any add-on resources, it is possible that some of the resources are not removed. This issue causes a subsequent add-on installation to fail.

items.title: CSCvz24357 - Kubernetes: Add-on URLs do not work
items.text: If more than 1 LoadBalancer IP address is assigned to a cluster, the add-on URL does not work.

items.title: CSCvz99130 - Kubernetes: Status and address information is missing from IKS inventory collection
items.text: The IKS inventory collection is dropping data about the node status and IP addresses due to misconfiguration of the inventory yaml.

items.title: CSCvz88105 - Kubernetes: Add-on installations fail with custom Namespace and Overrides configuration
items.text: Installation of the Dashboard, Monitor, and SMM add-on fails on tenant clusters that are configured with a custom Namespace or an Override settings.

items.title: CSCvy37909 - Kubernetes: essential-vsphere-csi custom resource fails
items.text: Occasionally, the essential-vsphere-csi HelmChart custom resource fails due to a race condition.

items.title: CSCwa47304 - Workload Optimizer: Log4j vulnerability
items.text: Intersight Assist Version 1.0.9-361 addresses the Log4j vulnerability.

items.title: CSCvz25142 - Kubernetes: Pagination issue in the Kubernetes cluster table
items.text: The Kubernetes cluster table displays 10 clusters at a time. Selection of clusters for a bulk action is limited to 10 because the next page, and previous page buttons are disabled.

items.title: CSCvw81794 - Kubernetes: Unable to parse / in the network name
items.text: While creating an Infrastructure Provider policy, IKS VM creation fails if you use the / in the input to the Interface field.

items.title: CSCvx27447 - Kubernetes: Push cached images to tenant registry
items.text: When you import a new VM template into vCenter, it has a name format that is shorter because “ubuntu” is replaced with “u”. This consumes additional storage space.

items.title: CSCvw50943 - Kubernetes: Incorrect style applied to Create button in the Infra Provider Policy page
items.text: The Create button in the Infra Provider Policy page is enabled even if the mandatory fields are not filled. Moreover, there are no error messages displayed when you click the Create button without entering the mandatory fields. This is a button styling issue.

items.title: CSCvx41865 - Intersight Managed Mode: The deployment of Server Profile fails with an error- UnconfigureSingleVeth failed with error : Commit request failed
items.text: After recommission or decommission of chassis, the deployment of server profile on a server may fail with an error - - UnconfigureSingleVeth failed with error : Commit request failed. This issue is resolved in Release 4.1(3c).

items.title: CSCvv23834 – Intersight Managed Mode: vHBA bound vETH's VLAN do not change after modification of FCoE VLAN, resulting in hosts losing SAN connection
items.text: When an FCoE VLAN of a VSAN is modified in the domain profile, the hosts that are using the VSAN lose SAN connectivity.

items.title: CSCwa76782 - Kubernetes: API DELETE of Kubernetes NodeGroup fails
items.text: If you issue a REST API DELETE for a Kubernetes NodeGroupProfile that has NodeProfiles in the Nodes property, you will see a 202 HTTP response (delete accepted). A workflow will be initiated, and it will conclude with a COMPLETED status, however, the NodeGroupProfile is not deleted.

items.title: CSCvx72042 - Kubernetes: Add-on data is not displayed in the Cluster Details page
items.text: The data of the deployed add-ons is not displayed in the Monitor and Operate tabs of the Cluster Details page.

items.title: CSCvx76462 - Kubernetes: Ingress IP address changes on scale or upgrade
items.text: The virtual IP address of Nginx ingress might not be retained after a tenant upgrade.

items.title: CSCvx61290 – HyperFlex: Cluster profile may not be automatically imported if a HyperFlex cluster has nodes with the same hostname
items.text: HyperFlex cluster import fails when the cluster deployed outside of the current account is claimed and if the cluster has nodes with duplicate hostnames.

items.title: CSCvx01762 - Intersight Managed Mode: Locator LED operation is not working for FEX
items.text: The FEX LED operation is not working from both the API and the GUI.

items.title: CSCvv81281 - Cloud Orchestration: Hitachi - Missing Data Reduction and Provisioned fields in main view
items.text: Data Reduction and Provisioned fields available under Intersight > Operate > Storage for Hitachi targets managed via Intersight display null values.

items.title: CSCvx40544 - HyperFlex: Server firmware upgrade for HyperFlex Edge clusters may fail when all servers in the cluster are not claimed in Intersight
items.text: An error message is now available in the Intersight UI to indicate that all the servers must be claimed for a successful server firmware upgrade.

items.title: CSCvw85728 - HyperFlex: HX Controller VM actions are not disabled by default for vCenter Targets, or, internal HX Datastore actions are not disabled by default for vCenter Targets
items.text: HX Controller VM actions are not disabled by default for vCenter Targets, or, internal HX Datastore actions are not disabled by default for vCenter Targets

items.title: CSCvx36564 - Infrastructure: iSCSI Boot policy creation fails with Auto Target Source and Auto Initiator IP Source options
items.text: When you create an iSCSI Boot policy with “Auto” target source type, policy creation fails with the following error “Failed to create a policy iSCSI Boot”. A similar error is seen when you create an iSCSI Boot policy with "Static" target source type with "Auto" Initiator IP Source.

items.title: CSCvx03750 - Infrastructure: Unable to create network connectivity policy with only IPv6 address
items.text: The user is unable to create a network connectivity policy exclusively with IPv6 address. This issue is found both in the user interface and in the API.

items.title: CSCvx13383 - Intersight Managed Mode: The default value of Tunneled vKVM is toggled to the on state on resetting the device connector
items.text: Tunneled vKVM is toggled to the off state for all servers to rectify this issue.

items.title: CSCvv64458 - Intersight Virtual Appliance: In the Cisco Intersight Virtual Appliance release, 1.0.9-164, UCS Firmware Upgrade images fail to display in the UI
items.text: UCS Firmware Upgrade images fail to display in the UI in the Cisco Intersight Virtual Appliance release, 1.0.9-164.

items.title: CSCvw48300 - Workload Optimizer: Editing a Dynatrace target does not pre-fill the current Environment ID parameter.
items.text: When editing a Dynatrace target, the Environment ID parameter that was filled during target claim is not displayed.

items.title: CSCvt61722 - Infrastructure: Unexpected errors are seen if System QoS policy is not selected during UCS Domain profile creation
items.text: UCS Domain profile creation for Intersight Managed targets are allowed without selecting System QoS Policy

items.title: CSCvv13330 - Infrastructure: User cannot NTP, Network Connectivity and Syslog policies for Domain Profile in an account at Cisco Intersight Base license
items.text: User cannot use NTP, Network Connectivity and Syslog policies for Domain Profile in an account at Cisco Intersight Base license.

items.title: CSCvs96324 - Infrastructure: Port policy configuration option is shown for Server Administrators
items.text: Although, a Server Administrator can edit and save a Port Policy, it will fail to deploy with  HTTP 403 forbidden error.

items.title: CSCvv12655 - HyperFlex: HyperFlex cluster deployment is not supported on Intersight Private Virtual Appliance
items.text: HyperFlex cluster deployment is not supported on Intersight Private Virtual Appliance releases, 1.0.9-148 and 1.0.9-150.

items.title: CSCvu52248 - HyperFlex: HyperFlex Cluster Capacity Runway may be shown as locked in the HyperFlex cluster table view due to insufficient license
items.text: For some HyperFlex clusters in the Essentials or higher licensing tier claimed before May 28th, 2020, the Capacity Runway may be shown as locked in the HyperFlex cluster table view due to miscalculated license.

items.title: CSCvt70211 - Infrastructure: The user label set does not work for blade servers
items.text: The user label set does not work for blade servers.

items.title: CSCvv27196 - Intersight Private Virtual Appliance: Tech Support collection is not initiated for endpoints
items.text: This caveat is applicable only for Intersight Private Virtual Appliance. Clicking on the UI action "Collect Tech Support" for Servers, Fabric Interconnects, and UCS Director targets does not initiate the tech support collection process.

items.title: CSCvv14616 - Intersight Managed Mode: Intersight Managed FI-attached rack server fails to discover in Intersight
items.text: Intersight Managed FI-attached rack server fails to discover in Intersight. The failure is at the "Enable Server Event Channel" workflow stage.

items.title: CSCvu39006 - Infrastructure: IPv6 HTTP file share of custom port mapping with DNS name gives an error
items.text: OS installations using an IPv6 HTTP file share with custom port mapping with the DNS name for vMedia mapping results in an error.

items.title: CSCvu01357 - Infrastructure: HTTP, HTTPS file shares to add support for non-default port number
items.text: OS installations using IPv6-based HTTP and HTTPS file shares for vMedia mapping results in an error.

items.title: CSCvr69139 - Infrastructure: Persistent Memory security operations
items.text: Persistent Memory security operations may fail when the passphrase contains the ’&’ symbol.

items.title: CSCvr88988 - Infrastructure: Boot Policy does not support non-VIC adapters for PXE boot configuration
items.text: Boot policy in Intersight requires an Interface name or MAC addresses for non-VIC adapters including the LOM and the adapters in PCI slots.

items.title: CSCvq80554 - Infrastructure: Stale or missing HCL data in Intersight
items.text: Stale or missing HCL data in Intersight due to a failure in the communication between the Cisco IMC and Cisco UCS Manager.

items.title: CSCvp88912 - Infrastructure: Persistent Memory Inventory not updated
items.text: In the Persistent Memory Inventory, Regions and Namespaces are not updated after configuration changes.

items.title: CSCvs49282 - HyperFlex: HX Cluster Capacity Runway is not displayed even when the servers are licensed under the Essentials tier
items.text: After a server belonging to a Fabric Interconnect-attached HyperFlex cluster gets decommissioned and recommissioned from Cisco UCS Manager, the license validation for the cluster reports an incorrect result. Therefore, the HyperFlex Cluster Capacity Runway is not displayed even when the servers are licensed under the Essentials tier.

items.title: CSCvp28844 - Infrastructure: Deployment of LAN Connectivity Policy on Cisco UCS C240 M5 Rack Servers with Quad Port adapters running on Cisco IMC 4.0(x) fails
items.text: Deployment of LAN Connectivity Policy on Cisco UCS C240 M5 Rack Servers with Quad Port adapters running on Cisco IMC 4.0(x) fails.

items.title: CSCvr88366 - Infrastructure: Cisco UCSC-MLOM-C25Q-04 driver is not listed in the Cisco UCS Hardware and Software Compatibility tool for Standalone UCS C240 M5S servers
items.text: Cisco UCSC-MLOM-C25Q-04 driver is not listed in the UCS Hardware and Software Compatibility tool for Standalone UCS C240 M5S servers with Intel Xenon Processor Scalable Family running Oracle Linux UEK 4.1.4.35-1902.

items.title: CSCvr88381 - Infrastructure: 12G Modular SAS RAID Controller is not listed in the Cisco UCS Hardware and Software Compatibility tool for Standalone UCS C240 M5S servers
items.text: Cisco 12G Modular SAS RAID Controller is not listed in the UCS Hardware and Software Compatibility tool for Standalone UCS C240 M5S servers with Intel Xenon Processor Scalable Family running Oracle Linux UEK 4.1.12-112 and Oracle Linux UEK 4.1.12-124, on Cisco IMC 4.0(4).

items.title: CSCvr26096 - Infrastructure: LSI Mega Raid SAS 3108 and UCSC-MLOM-C40Q-03 driver is not listed in the Cisco UCS Hardware and Software Compatibility tool for Standalone Cisco UCS C240 M5S servers
items.text: LSI Mega Raid SAS 3108 and UCSC-MLOM-C40Q-03 driver is not listed in the in the UCS Hardware and Software Compatibility tool for Standalone UCS C240 M5S servers running Oracle Linux UEK 4.1.4.35-1902, on Cisco IMC 4.0(4).

items.title: CSCvo05293 - Infrastructure: Deployment of Network Policies fails intermittently on Cisco UCS C-Series Standalone M4 and M5 servers
items.text: Deployment of LAN Connectivity, SAN Connectivity, and Adapter Configuration Policies on Cisco UCS C-Series Standalone M4 and M5 servers running Cisco IMC version 4.0(2c) fails intermittently. This condition is caused when there are more than 2 Cisco VIC (Virtual Interface Card) Adapters on the server, and the policies are deployed on all of them.

items.title: CSCwi35170 - Intersight Managed Mode: IMT Check Failure for FlexPod due to Cisco's New IMM Mode FI Firmware Version Format
items.text: Cisco's new version format for the Intersight Managed Mode Fabric Interconnect firmware is causing the IMT check for FlexPod to fail. NetApp is addressing this issue with an AIQUM update to match the new format.
items.workaround: None.

items.title: CSCwh89681 - Intersight Managed Mode: Cloning of LAN Connectivity Policy (LCP) does not attach shared Network Group policy
items.text: When cloning LCP, if the Network Group policy attached to the vNIC is from a shared organization, the cloned LCP vNIC does not have the Network Group policy attached.
items.workaround: Manually edit the cloned LCP and attach the shared Network Group policy to the vNIC or vNICs.

items.title: CSCwf84266 - Intersight Managed Mode: Fabric Extender (FEX) inventory is not supported for UCSM Managed Mode (UMM)
items.text: Fabric Extender (FEX) inventory is currently supported for Intersight Managed Mode (IMM) only and not for UCSM Managed Mode (UMM).
items.workaround: None.

items.title: Monitoring RBAC: A Server Administrator is able to view Server or Fabric Interconnects metrics belonging to a different Organization
items.text: A server administrator may have the ability to access and monitor the metrics of Server or Fabric Interconnects that are owned by a different Organization.
items.workaround: None.

items.title: CSCwf78663 - Intersight Managed Mode: Number of adapter ports displayed in slot 2 of full width blade servers are incorrect.
items.text:
Full width blade with MLOM + Mezz1 + Mezz2 with UCSB-VIC-M84-4P}adapter, mezz2 slot in chassis topology displays 4 ports instead of 8 ports in Chassis having UCS-IOM-2208XP IOM.
Full-width blade with MLOM + PT + Mezz2 with UCS-IOM-2408 IOM displays 4 ports in each slot instead of 2 ports in each slot, representing 40G for MLOM+PT and 40G for Mezz 2 for each IOMs.
Half width blade with MLOM + PT (UCSB-MLOM-40G-03 cruz adapter ) with UCS-IOM-2208XP displays 4 ports instead of 8 ports in the adapter slot.
items.workaround: To redisplay the HIF port information, perform either decommissioning, recommissioning, or rediscovery of the chassis or blade.

items.title: CSCwf80217 - Intersight Managed Mode: Incorrectly displayed port count for UCS-IOM-2204XP.
items.text: IOM model UCS-IOM-2204XP has only 16 HIF ports, but API and GUI incorrectly displays 32 ports.

items.title: CSCwf89869 - Intersight Managed Mode: The HIF ports connection links are not displayed in the Topology view.
items.text: Even when the HIF ports of UCS-IOM-2304 are enabled and are in the Up state, the connection links are not shown in the Topology view.

items.title: CSCwf35723 - Intersight Managed Mode: Out-Of-Band IP retrieves the Fabric Interconnect device data instead of Server device data
items.text: When SNMP Policies are deployed on both Fabric Interconnects and server in a same default SNMP port (161), all the SNMP Walk requests using the server Out-Of-Band IP retrieves the Fabric Interconnect device data instead of the server device data. This issue further demands new forwarding rules for SNMP Walks.
items.workaround: Use any port number other than the standard default SNMP port (161) on the server for SNMP configuration.

items.title: CSCwf81672 - Intersight Managed Mode: Profile state is indicated as Pending when IDs are preserved
items.text: When the LAN identities (MAC, IQN, and iSCSI IP) or SAN identities (WWPN and WWNN) are preserved during modifying policies using profiles or templates, the profile state is indicated as Pending. This behavior occurs even if no other configuration changes are made.
items.workaround: To restore the profile status to OK, redeploy the profile.

items.title: CSCwf00718 - Intersight Managed Mode: MoDeepCloner API support in API docs
items.text: Currently, MoDeepCloner API can be used only if the payload has the required HTTP request header (prefer: respond-async).
items.workaround: Use the third-party tool with prefer: respond-async as the HTTP request header.

items.title: CSCwe89331 - Intersight Managed Mode: SCU overwrites data when an incorrect LUN ID is used for iSCSI LUN
items.text: Server Configuration Utility (SCU) overwrites data when an incorrect LUN ID is used for iSCSI LUN. For example: When a LUN ID "1" is specified for configuring the Server Profile and LUN ID "2" is specified for mapping iSCSI LUN on SAN storage, the data is overwritten on the discovered iSCSI LUN with LUN ID "2" and further results in data loss. This issue is seen on 6.2.2a, 6.2.2b, and 6.2.3b SCU versions.
items.workaround:
To avoid this issue, do one of the following:
•) Use the same LUN ID in the Server Profile and in the SAN Storage where the OS needs to be installed.
•) Use 6.3.1a SCU version.

items.title: CSCwe97289 - Intersight Managed Mode: Post install of SUSE Linux Enterprise  Server 15 SP4 OS, server enters into Dracut shell on iSCSI LUN targets
items.text: After the installation of SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 OS on FI-attached Cisco UCS-C220-M7 and Cisco UCS-C240-M7 servers in Intersight Managed Mode, the server enters into Dracut shell instead of booting to the OS. This issue is seen only on iSCSI LUN targets.
items.workaround: None.

items.title: CSCwe81948 - Intersight Managed Mode: fnic Driver version and protocol is not getting listed under HCL tab on ESXi 8.0
items.text: For VIC 15231 MLOM on X210c M7 Compute Node, VIC adapter fnic Driver version and protocol is not getting listed under HCL Validation> Adapter Compliance tab.
items.workaround: None.

items.title: CSCwd82960 - Intersight Managed Mode: ComputeNotConnected alarm does not clear when blade discovery fails
items.text: When IMM blade discovery fails in the first task, an alarm (Name = ‘ComputeNotConnected’) is raised to alert the user of the failure. As per the design, there will be no entry in the server list on the UI in this case, so there is no way to clear this alarm through UI.
items.workaround: User will be able to clear the alarm by doing a ‘remove’ operation on the server via API.

items.title: CSCwd14586 - Intersight Managed Mode: For UCS-IOM-2304 and UCS-IOM-2304V2 IO Modules, after adding or deleting no-drop class, IOM reboot is required
items.text: When the no-drop class in System QoS policy is changed after deploying the Domain Profile on the Fabric Interconnects,  all UCS-IOM-2304 IO Modules on both sides of the Fabric Interconnect need to be reset (IOM reset will reboot the IOM) for the QoS changes to be applied.
items.workaround: None.

items.title: CSCwe08601 - Intersight Managed Mode: Chassis profile deployment fails after IOM reset, decommission, or recommission
items.text: After the reset, decommission, or recommission activity on the IOM (UCS-IOM-2304V or UCS-IOM-2304V2), the IOM displays online state, but the Chassis (UCSB-5108) profile deployment fails with this error message "Unable to apply the configuration because the IOM-B is offline. Retry when the IOM is online. If the issue persists, contact Cisco TAC.
items.workaround: Reboot the IOM and retry the Chassis profile deployment soon after the IOM comes online.

items.title: CSCwd53385 - Intersight Managed Mode: OS boot fails in Cisco UCS B200 M6 server after chassis power-cycle
items.text: When power is lost and restored on a chassis, the server boot to the host OS fails when booting via FC LUN. It is due to a delay in configuring the HIF ports of the adapter. The host tries to boot but has no path, so it fails and boots into the UEFI shell instead.
items.workaround: The user should manually reboot the host after the discovery completes.

items.title: CSCwc54734 - Intersight Managed Mode: vNIC status is not operational.
items.text: Faults are not displayed for all vNICs after a reboot in Intersight Managed Mode. This is either due to the Server Profile that has vNICs are pinned to an uplink which is down or the vNICs are not pinned to any uplink or host OS down.
items.workaround: Operational state of the vEthernet corresponding to the vNIC should be validated from the NXOS CLI.

items.title: CSCwd14758 - Intersight Managed Mode: Cisco UCS VIC 1380 adapter does not get updated on moving a Cisco UCS B-Series M5 server from UMM to IMM
items.text: A Cisco UCS B-Series M5 server having firmware version 4.2(2a) is migrated from UMM to IMM. Cisco UCS 1400 Series adapters are replaced with Cisco UCS 1300 Series adapters. After the discovery of the server, Cisco UCS VIC 1340 adapter gets updated, but Cisco UCS VIC 1380 does not get updated.
items.workaround: Upgrade Cisco IMC firmware to 4.2(2e) to support auto-upgrade of Cisco UCS VIC 1300 Series adapters.

items.title: CSCwd76511 - Intersight Managed Mode: Server discovery failure due to adapter issues
items.text: Server discovery fails for a B-Series or C-Series M5 server with Cisco UCS VIC 1300 Series adapters. The GUI shows message as "No Adapter Units found. Try a rediscovery of the server after sometime." The possible causes can be adapter not working, connectivity issues with the adapter, unsupported Cisco IMC firmware version.
items.workaround: Upgrade Cisco IMC firmware to 4.2(2e) for B-Series server, Cisco IMC firmware to 4.2(2g) for C-Series server, and VIC firmware to 4.5(2d).

items.title: CSCwd16403 - Intersight Managed Mode: The Hostname displays as Linux after SUSE Linux Enterprise Server (SLES) Operating System (OS) 12 SP5 version install
items.text: After SLES OS install, the Hostname is displayed as Linux by default instead of SLES. This is specifically observed on SLES OS 12 SP5 version
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCwc91572 - Intersight Managed Mode: Modifications to domain profile PVLAN configuration are not reflected in the server profile PVLAN
items.text: When a domain profile PVLAN is modified, for example, mapping the Isolated or Community VLAN to a different Primary VLAN, the changes are not reflected in the server profile PVLAN.
items.workaround: After modifying the PVLAN configuration and deploying the domain profile, redeploy the server profile as well.

items.title: CSCwc40084 - Intersight Managed Mode: Domain profile deployment fails when a VLAN with the same ID as a residual FCoE VLAN is used in VLAN configuration
items.text: Deployment of the domain profile fails, when a VLAN with the same ID as a residual FCoE VLAN, is used in VLAN configuration. Deployment of the domain profile fails when the FCoE VLAN, created during previous operations, is not cleared from the Fabric Interconnects.
items.workaround: Deploy a domain profile with an empty VSAN policy.

items.title: CSCvz51265 - Intersight Managed Mode: Scale Issue: Internal error occurs while deleting policies
items.text: The operation to delete an Ethernet Network policy with 3000 VLANs leads to Internal Error in the first attempt. Deleting an Ethernet Network Policy with 3000 VLANs triggers a cascade delete and DB operations of the VLANs leading to Internal Error.
items.workaround: Retry the operation.

items.title: CSCwc62086 - Intersight Managed Mode: UEFI Secure Boot not supported on ESXi Operating System for Standalone and IMM servers
items.text: In Cisco UCS C-Series Standalone and Intersight Managed Mode servers, the UEFI Secure Boot is not supported on ESXi Operating System Installation.
items.workaround: The Secure Boot is disabled during the ESXi OS install workflow and is re-enabled after the installation is complete.

items.title: CSCwc35483 - Intersight Managed Mode: UEFI Secure Boot not supported on RHEL Operating System for IMM servers
items.text: For RHEL installation with UEFI Secure Boot on Intersight Managed Mode (IMM) servers, the keys used for signing LSI storage drivers are to be added manually in the Machine Owner Key (MOK) database for booting the OS after installation.
items.workaround:
Do the following:
1)Set UEFI secure boot as No.
2)Install OS with signed async driver.(If OS is already installed, ignore this step)
3)Boot the OS.
4)Add to the MOK database the key. Key available with BRCM driver release. “mokutil –import DCSG00411462_selfcert.der”.
5)Shutdown the host.
6)Set the UEFI Secure Boot as Yes.
7)Boot the host.
8)In the boot prompt, you will receive a prompt Press any key enroll the key. Press any key.
9) Select Enroll the key and click Yes to acknowledge the key addition.
10) Boot the host in Linux.

items.title: CSCwa85888 - Intersight Managed Mode: Domain Profile redeploy and port configuration fails after erase-configuration for Cisco UCS 6400 Series FI
items.text: On re-deploying a domain profile after performing the erase configuration, Ethernet related port configuration is not pushed to Fabric Interconnect device connector for 6400 Series FI.
items.workaround:
If this issue occurs, do the following:
1)Create empty Port policy (without any configuration).
2)Change the Port policy for the domain profile to empty policy.
3)Deploy the domain profile, this will clear all sw.* MOs in IMM.
4)Change the Port policy for domain profile to the original policy.
5)Deploy the domain profile again, this step should push all the necessary configuration to the FI.
6)Check and verify configuration from NXOS shell on FI.

items.title: CSCwa77455 - Intersight Managed Mode: Server discovery is not initiated on dynamically adding servers to N9K-C93180YC-FX3 Fabric Extender
items.text: Re-claim and re-deploy the domain profile whose servers were discovered through N9K-C93180YC-FX3 FEX. FEX discovers both the Fabric Interconnects but the discovery of servers connected using 25G cables is not initiated.
items.workaround: Rediscover the Fabric Extender.

items.title: CSCwa80829 - Intersight Managed Mode: VNIC/VHBA configuration is not restored for N9K-C93180YC-FX3 Fabric Extender connected server after decom/recom fex
items.text: N9K-C93180YC-FX3 Fabric Extender is first decommissioned, then re-commissioned and discovered. VNIC/VHBA configuration is not restored after FEX re-discovery.
items.workaround: Deploy the server profile after FEX recommissioning.

items.title: CSCwa34969 - Intersight Managed Mode: Switch mode changes are not reflected in the Fabric Interconnect inventory immediately after the Fabric Interconnect reboots
items.text: When the switching mode is changed, the Fabric Interconnect reboots, but the switch mode changes are sometimes not reflected in the Fabric Interconnect inventory.
items.workaround: This issue occurs intermittently and the Fabric Interconnect sets itself right after sometime.

items.title: CSCvz41858 - Intersight Managed Mode: On rebooting a server after successful Windows installation with iSCSI Boot and UEFI, the server boot is getting stuck at the EFI shell.
items.text: When a server profile is configured with UEFI + iSCSI Boot and during the installation if the custom partitioning of LUN is selected, the boot loader is installed on the M.2 RAID controller disks or local disks, if present. The server boot after the Windows installation is getting stuck at the EFI shell.
items.workaround:
To avoid booting of server getting stuck at the EFI shell, do one of the following:
• During the installation, do not select the NEW option for the custom disk partitioning
• Type EXIT in the EFI shell to have the Windows loaded
• Before the installation, remove M.2 RAID controller disks or local disks, manually

items.title: CSCvy97064 - Intersight Managed Mode: Disk numbers cannot be swapped between different Drive Groups and between Drive Group and Global Hot Spare.
items.text: Updating a Storage policy by swapping the disk numbers shows an update failure message.
items.workaround: The user has to edit the policy in two steps. First, the user needs to remove the disk number from the dedicated hot spare and save the policy. Next, the user needs to add this disk number to the Global Hot Spare and again save the policy.

items.title: CSCvy00949 - Intersight Managed Mode: Unable to swap disk slots between Drive Group and Global Hot Spare, and between different Drive Groups
items.text: While editing a storage policy, an error occurs when the user tries to swap a disk slot from Drive Group to Global Hot Spare OR when they try to swap a disk slot from one Drive Group to another.
items.workaround: None.

items.title: CSCvz14187 – Intersight Managed Mode: The Replace workflow status of the cluster Fabric Interconnect displays the workflow state as Unknown
items.text: The Replace workflow status of the cluster Fabric Interconnect is displayed as Unknown when the workflow is a success
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCvx85657 – Intersight Managed Mode: Reset CMOS does not preserve the Boot policy and Bios policy settings
items.text: CMOS reset operation does not preserve the configuration settings of Boot and BIOS policies on the server.
items.workaround: Manually re-deploy the server profile(s) containing these policies after the completion of CMOS reset operation.

items.title: CSCvz04956 – Intersight Managed Mode: Switch role is not supported in IdP initiated SSO
items.text: Switching of user role is not supported when a user with multiple accounts or multiple roles is logged in using IdP initiated SSO.
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCvx70944 — Intersight Managed Mode: OS installation together on multiple servers fails intermittently
items.text: When you select multiple servers together for OS install, then OS installation fails sometimes.
items.workaround: Cancel the steps and then retry the same.

items.title: CSCvx73658 – Intersight Managed Mode: For IMM c240 rack servers, the field-replaceable-unit fan module 7 is shown as missing
items.text: c240 specification has six fan modules. Fan module 7 should not be shown as missing.
items.workaround: Perform a manual rediscover on the rack server to clear the fan module 7 missing alarm.

items.title: CSCvy09951 – Intersight Managed Mode: IMM expects both the Fabric interconnects to be available for implementing configuration changes through domain profile, chassis profile, and server profile
items.text: IMM expects both the Fabric interconnects to be available for implementing configuration changes through domain profile, chassis profile, and server profile.
items.workaround: Retry the profile deployment when both the Fabric Interconnects are available online.

items.title: CSCwb47181 - UCSM Managed Mode:  Intersight HCL shows incomplete for compliance with UCSM servers and Cisco Custom VMWare ESXi OS installed
items.text: UCSM servers are missing the host inventory annotations. Some of the values in the key-value pairs are mismatched or missing.
items.workaround: Reset the CIMC and manually trigger the inventory from VMWare ESXi OS.

items.title: CSCvu70337 - UCSM Managed Mode: The user is unable to trigger any workflow if the UMM upgrade fails once
items.text: Once the user starts an upgrade, the supported hardware becomes incompatible. Even after decommissioning the hardware, Cisco Intersight picks up the last failure state. Hence, it throws a validation error.
items.workaround: The user first needs to decommission the unsupported hardware and then trigger an upgrade from Cisco UCS Manager to clear the previous state.

items.title: CSCwf87413 - M6 expansion failure during vNIC configuration: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'dn'
items.text: When installing or expanding a HyperFlex cluster with UCS M6 servers, the Intersight installer fails during the "Configure UCS server" task when the vNIC configuration times out.
items.workaround: Reboot the Cisco Integrated Management Controller (CIMC) and retry the installation/expansion workflow.

items.title: CSCwh07204 - HyperFlex: Estimate generation failure error message is incomplete
items.text:
If the expansion recommendation has been generated for a cluster before the feature became generally available, the estimate generation for the recommendation will fail. The following error message displays:
There was an error while generating estimate. Possible causes are A) Cisco Commerce Workspace is unavailable at this time, B) Items in the recommendation are not available for ordering or C) The access token used for generating the estimate is invalid. Please fix the issues and try again.
items.workaround: Trigger the cluster expansion recommendation again using either the same or different values for the expansion request.

items.title: CSCwh03430 - HyperFlex: Estimate generation fails after multiple retries
items.text:
While generating estimation for recommended hardware in Cluster Expansion Planning, estimate generation fails even after multiple retries and the following error message displays:
There was an error while generating estimate. Possible causes are A) Cisco Commerce Workspace is unavailable at this time, B) Items in the recommendation are not available for ordering or C) The access token used for generating the estimate is invalid. Please fix the issues and try again.
items.workaround:
Using the Intersight APIs (https://intersight.com/apidocs), delete the current access token as follows:
1) Obtain "Moid" for the current user from the iam/User API endpoint. Look for the "Name" property to ensure that it is the same user.
2) Obtain "Moid" for the access token to be deleted, by filtering a query on the API oauth/AccessTokens. Filter to use: TokenOwner eq `user_moid` and ApiType eq `CommerceEstimate-API`.
3) Delete AccessToken with the "Moid" retrieved from Step 2 using API oauth/AccessTokens.
4) Regenerate the estimate from the Cluster Expansion Recommendation feature.

items.title: CSCwf41388 - HyperFlex: Expansion of an imported cluster with compute nodes fails during the 'Validate Cluster Configuration Before Expansion' step.
items.text: Expanding an imported HyperFlex cluster in Intersight fails with the error message "Error validating cluster expansion for cluster profile <cluster profile name>". This defect only affects expanding imported HyperFlex clusters using Intersight. An "imported" cluster is a cluster that was deployed outside of Intersight or using a different Intersight account than the one being used for the expansion operation. The issue is only present if a cluster with compute nodes was imported and then subsequently expanded in Intersight. Imported clusters with only converged nodes are not affected. Imported clusters that were claimed with only converged nodes and then subsequently expanded with compute nodes also do not exhibit this issue.
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCwa79851 - HyperFlex: Upgrade ESXi on all nodes to 7.0U2 or above and retry
items.text: For FI-attached clusters, the following error may be seen during the Hypervisor management network configuration step, even if all of the nodes are running the same ESXi version: Please upgrade ESXi on all nodes to 7.0U2 or above and retry.
items.workaround: Click retry and it will go through without failing again. Note that several retries may be required before the workflow proceeds past the point of failure.

items.title: CSCvy94937 - HyperFlex: HyperFlex cluster expansion for compute nodes fails with error message: Insufficient Replication IPs
items.text: For a HyperFlex cluster in which Backup policy is deployed, cluster expansion may fail due to insufficient replication IPs.
items.workaround:
To recover from this failure, do the following:
1) Abort the expansion workflow.
2) Remove the expanded node
3) Deploy the Backup Policy to increase the IP range.

items.title: CSCvz06219 - HyperFlex: After HyperFlex cluster expansion datastore displays ‘Mount Failure’ status
items.text: After HyperFlex cluster expansion when an existing backup target datastore is mounted on newly added node of the target HyperFlex cluster, the datastore displays ‘Mount Failure’ status.
items.workaround: Unmount the existing target datastore from the newly added host.

items.title: CSCvy84658 - HyperFlex: Upgrading ESXi to 7.0 U2 may fail on servers running older generation CPUs
items.text: HyperFlex cluster upgrade initiated through Intersight or HX Connect will display the failure message "CPU_SUPPORT WARNING: The CPU in this host may not be supported in future ESXi releases. Please plan accordingly."
items.workaround:
To recover from this failure, do the following:
1) Exit the host from maintenance mode and restore the health of the HyperFlex Data Platform storage cluster.
2) Ensure that HyperFlex Data Platform is upgraded to the same version across all converged nodes in the cluster.
3) If some nodes in the cluster were upgraded to 7.0 U2, manually upgrade the remaining nodes in the cluster.

items.title: CSCwc27500 - HPE ProLiant Servers: Some of the adapters are not shown in inventory
items.text: This issue is seen when the HPE ProLiant servers have HP or Broadcom adapters. Redfish API response from the server does not return the details for these adapters because of which Intersight server inventory is not able to display them.
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCwc17410 - Dell PowerEdge Servers: Mismatch in the number of storage controllers between BMC UI and Intersight Server Inventory UI
items.text: This issue is seen due to the discrepancy in the number of storage controllers returned in the Redfish API response.
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCwc17404 - Dell PowerEdge Servers: Mismatch in the number of PCIe devices between iDRAC UI and Intersight Server Inventory UI
items.text: This issue is seen due to the discrepancy in the number of PCIe devices returned in the Redfish API response.
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCwh05933 - Workload Optimizer: ServiceNow (SNOW) target stuck in 'claim in progress' status.
items.text: In some circumstances, a SNOW target may indefinitely stay in "claim in progress" status when being claimed.
items.workaround: Delete all SNOW policies for the IWO target and then unclaim and re-claim the target.

items.title: CSCvx72064 - Workload Optimizer: In the Workload Optimizer supply chain HyperFlex DiskArray and Hyper-V storage may not display some values
items.text: In the Workload Optimizer supply chain, HyperFlex DiskArray will not display Hyper-V storage, attached hosts, and virtual machine; likewise Hyper-V cluster will not display HyperFlex DiskArray.
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCwf86010 - After unclaim all targets search/SearchItems API still have transceivers MOs of deleted target.
items.text: In the EMEA region, after deleting all targets, filter with the deleted RegisteredDevice Moid in /api/v1/search/SearchItems returns two equipment.
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCwe42476 - Intersight Cloud Orchestrator: vSphere 8.0 fails to display error messages in the Pre-Check status
items.text:
Firmware Upgrade using Cisco Hardware Support Manager (HSM) enabled with vSphere Lifecycle Manager (vLCM) plug-in supports UCSM Managed B-Series and C-series servers only with Local Service profiles and Initial Templates. When the firmware upgrade is attempted with Global Service Profile or Updating Templates, the Pre-Check status fails to display one of the following error messages in vLCM:
• Service Profile of server is bound to a template of type updating-template. Upgrade operation is supported only with the initial-template.
• The associated service profile is not owned by UCS Manager. Change the service profile ownership to local and re-trigger the upgrade. This issue is observed only on VMware vSphere 8.0 version.
items.workaround: If the Pre-Check error messages are not displayed in vLCM, you can check the Server Profile workflow (Validate Firmware Upgrade) status or error messages in Cisco Intersight.

items.title: CSCwa92321 - Intersight Cloud Orchestrator: Assigning a value to the workflow variable using the transformed mapping template option does not work
items.text: When you use the transformed mapping template option to assign a value to the workflow variable, the transformed mapping rule does not get applied during workflow execution. Instead, the initial value of the variable is mapped.
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCwb41333 - Intersight Cloud Orchestrator: Require proper validation of outputs/inputs used in condition
items.text: When Condition is used as the loop mode in a Serial Loop task, the evaluation of the condition fails if the task name or input/output names are wrong, and the loop exits after the first iteration without honoring the condition.
items.workaround: Copy the task name or input/output names from the code view or from the WorkflowDefinition API GET call to avoid mistakes while defining the condition.

items.title: CSCwb83887 - Kubernetes: Cluster profile deployment fails if the Port for the vCenter Target is set to 0
items.text: If the Port for the vCenter Target is set to 0, cluster profile deployment fails with the following error: Missing Port for vCenter target specified in VirtualMachineInfraConfigPolicy my-infra-policy. Check Target configuration and retry.
items.workaround:
1. From the left pane of intersight.com, go to Admin > Targets.
2. Ensure that Port has a value other than zero
- If Port = 0 and the connection type is secure, update 0 to 443.
- If Port = 0 and the connection type is insecure, update 0 to 80.
This action will start a workflow in Requests, and the status in the Targets view will be updated with the success or failure of the updated connection properties.

items.title: CSCwb32462 - Kubernetes: Nvidia-device-plugin pods fail to start after a cluster upgrade
items.text: IKS Images 1.20.14-iks.0 and later do not cache the container image required for the nvidia-device-plugin daemonset. Not caching the image results in the nvidia-device-plugin pods remaining in the ImagePullBackOff state and the pods failing to start.
items.workaround:
A cluster does not use the nvidia-device-plugin daemonset. You can delete the daemonset with the following command:
kubectl -n kube-system delete ds nvidia-device-plugin-daemonset

items.title: CSCwa17374 - Kubernetes: Unable to reconfigure some of the IKS settings after initial cluster deployment
items.text:
You can configure the following settings only during the initial cluster deployment:
- Container Runtime policy
- Virtual Machine Infra Config policy
- Virtual Machine Instance Type policy
- IP Pools
- Trusted Registries policy
- SSH keys
Any change to these settings thereafter will not be applied to the cluster.
items.workaround: There is no known workaround for this issue.

items.title: CSCwa92460 - Kubernetes: Upgrading the Monitoring add-on to 0.4.11-cisco1 or later fails and creates multiple Monitoring jobs
items.text: Upgrading the Monitoring add-on to 0.4.11-cisco1 or later fails and creates multiple Monitoring jobs.
items.workaround: For more information on the workaround, see the IKS User Guide > Open Caveats section.

items.title: CSCwa51692 - Kubernetes: Upgrading 1.19.15 IKS tenant with SMM addon times out and fails
items.text: 1.19.15 IKS tenant upgrade fails when you install the SMM add-on and then try to upgrade the tenant.
items.workaround:
1. In the 1.19.15 IKS tenant, open the cp-v111x istiocontrolplane CR in the istio-system namespace:
kubectl edit istiocontrolplane cp-v111x -n istio-system -o yaml
2. Edit the cp-v111x istiocontrolplane CR and set the following field:
spec.istiod.deployment.podDisruptionBudget.minAvailable=0
3. Save the cp-v111x istiocontrolplane CR.
4. Upgrade the tenant.

items.title: CSCvz89618 - Kubernetes: SMM add-on installation fails with Docker proxy configuration
items.text: Installation of SMM add-on fails on tenant clusters that are configured with docker proxy settings.
items.workaround: There is no workaround for this issue.

items.title: CSCvz24357 - Kubernetes: Add-on URLs do not work
items.text: If more than 1 LoadBalancer IP address is assigned to a cluster, the add-on URL does not work.
items.workaround:
1. Obtain the Ingress IP address.
2. Change the IP address in your add-on URL to the Ingress IP address from Step 1.

items.title: CSCvy10553 - Kubernetes: Unable to configure VM of disk size greater than 40GB
items.text: When you configure a VM of disk size greater than 40GB, the system defaults to a disk size of 40 GB.
items.workaround: There is no workaround for this issue.

items.title: CSCvx97900 - Kubernetes: Certs validation is not supported
items.text: Validation for invalid and expired certs is not supported.
items.workaround: Ensure that you input a valid certificate with a maximum validity period.

items.title: CSCvw96794 - Kubernetes: IKS cluster deployment fails if ESX clusters use a resource pool with the same name across multiple datacenters
items.text: In a given datacenter, if multiple ESX clusters use resource pools having the same name then deployment of IKS clusters that use this resource pool fails.
items.workaround: Ensure that the resource pool name is unique across ESX clusters and datacenters.

items.title: CSCwe75129 - Infrastructure: Disabled IPv6 in Network Connectivity policy is not yet supported for Cisco UCS C240 M7 server in Standalone mode
items.text: A Network Connectivity policy is created by disabling the IPv6 field. A Server Profile for Cisco UCS C240 Standalone M7 server is created and the created Network Connectivity policy is attached to it. The profile is deployed. Server Profile drift is observed after the deployment.
items.workaround: None.

items.title: CSCwf04341 - Infrastructure: For Cisco UCS C240 Standalone M7 server, drift is observed on Syslog policy when both the Syslog Servers are disabled in the policy
items.text: A Syslog policy is created by disabling both the Syslog servers. A Server Profile for Cisco UCS C240 Standalone M7 server is created and the created Syslog policy is attached to it. The profile is deployed. Server Profile drift is observed after the deployment.
items.workaround: None.

items.title: CSCwf12151 - Infrastructure: Server Profile status is Out of Sync and the PXE boot device is not updated in the end point server when no Port value is provided
items.text: A Boot Order policy is created with PXE boot device by selecting the Port and keeping the Port value as empty. A Server Profile for C-Series Standalone M7 server is created and the created Boot Order policy is attached to it. The profile is deployed and the configurations are pushed to the endpoint server. The Server Profile status goes out of sync and the PXE boot device does not get updated.
items.workaround: Provide the Port number in the Port field.

items.title: CSCwe89427 - Infrastructure: Existing LDAP groups are not getting updated for C-Series Standalone M7 server
items.text: An LDAP group is manually added by logging into C-Series M7 Standalone server through CIMC UI. An LDAP policy is then created in Intersight with new LDAP groups. A Server Profile for C-Series Standalone M7 server is created and the created LDAP policy is attached to it. The profile is deployed. It is observed that the profile goes out of sync and the existing LDAP group is not replaced by the new ones.
items.workaround: None.

items.title: CSCwf16957 - Infrastructure: Redfish API for Nvme disk returns disk state as Good even though the disk is missing for Cisco UCS C220 M7 server in Standalone mode
items.text: In Cisco UCS C220 Standalone M7 server setup, it is observed that even when the presence of the physical disk is missing, the value of the health attribute is shown as OK.
items.workaround: Ignore the health attribute in case the value of the “Presence” attribute appears as “missing” in the following Intersight API: /api/v1/storage/PhysicalDisks.

items.title: CSCwb19524 - Infrastructure: Image download fails when you trigger, from Intersight, an upgrade of the UCSM infrastructure to the 4.2(1l)A bundle
items.text: UCS firmware download fails for the 4.2(1l) packages.
items.workaround: Retry the upgrade operation or upgrade to a different A bundle.

items.title: CSCvy83825 - Infrastructure: An Organization user is unable to perform bulk target claim
items.text: In the Claim Target page, an Organization user is unable to perform bulk target claim using Set the Configuration via File option.
items.workaround: Use the Set the Configuration via Wizard to perform the target claim

items.title: CSCvm67025 - Infrastructure: Unable to install Windows on a JBOD or virtual disk if these targets contain an existing Windows version
items.text: Unable to install Windows on a JBOD or virtual disk if these targets contain an existing Windows version.
items.workaround: Initialize or recreate the virtual drives before triggering installation.

items.title: CSCvo06115 - Infrastructure: OS Installation and Server Profile deployment cannot be performed simultaneously on the same server
items.text: OS installation and server profile deployment cannot be performed simultaneously on the same server. When the OS installation is in progress, power actions on the server are also disallowed.
items.workaround: Complete the server profile deployment and then initiate the OS installation. Do not perform power operations on the server during the OS installation.

items.title: CSCvq43037 - Infrastructure: Unable to install Windows Server OS, if the host already has a Windows Server OS installed on it
items.text: Unable to redeploy or reinstall Windows Server OS using the embedded mode of installation without Server Configuration Utility (SCU), if another Windows Server OS is already deployed on the same host.
items.workaround: Use SCU for the embedded mode of installation.

items.title: CSCvs09817 - Infrastructure: Persistent Memory shows 41 Namespaces only
items.text: Persistent Memory Inventory shows 41 Namespaces only, out of the 64 Namespaces configured.
items.workaround: In the next 24 hour inventory update, the Persistent Memory Inventory will be updated to show the 64 Namespaces.

items.title: CSCvs34452 - Infrastructure: Unable to view vCenter in Device Connector Manager for custom organizations with specific privileges
items.text: vCenter is not visible in Device Connector Manager for a custom organization with Server Administrator, HyperFlex Administrator, and Read-Only privileges. However, vCenter appears in the devices list.
items.workaround: None

items.title: CSCvt21720 - Infrastructure: Deployed Secure Passphrase window shows up during Server Profile deployment
items.text: When you change the Persistent Memory policy to a different one in the Server Profile and use the same Secure Passphrase for Persistent Memory Local Security, the Deployed Secure Passphrase window shows up during Server Profile deployment.
items.workaround: Reenter the Secure Passphrase for the Persistent Memory Policy.

items.title: CSCvt39285 - Infrastructure: RHEL8 installation is not supported on C125 M5 server with 4.0(2h) BIOS and firmware
items.text: When you install RHEL 8 with 4.0(2h) server firmware, the server becomes unresponsive.
items.workaround: When installing RHEL 8, use 4.1(1b) and above server firmware instead of 4.0(2h).

items.title: CSCvt62202 - Infrastructure: User roles modified in the GUI do not update in the backend
items.text: User roles modified in the GUI do not update in the backend.
items.workaround: There is no workaround. This is a known issue.

items.title: CSCvu84717 - Infrastructure: Special characters in file share like space and % can render a Bad Request format for OS installation
items.text: Special characters in file share like space and % can render a Bad Request format for OS installation.
items.workaround: Eliminate special characters from the requests sent to the file share.

items.title: CSCvv16489 - Infrastructure: The API gateway returns a timeout error
items.text: The API gateway returns a timeout error when 3000 VLANs are created at a time under the VLAN policy.
items.workaround: The recommended workaround is to create 100 VLANs at a time under the VLAN policy. All the defined VLANs can be deployed to the switch via Domain Profile deployed in one operation.

items.title: CSCvv81399 - Infrastructure: When using firmware 4.1(1x), the Install Target Selection page does not display a created virtual drive that is out of sync
items.text: When using firmware 4.1(1x), the Install Target Selection page does not display a created virtual drive that is out of sync.
items.workaround: To avoid synchronization problems with virtual drives, use firmware versions 4.1(2a) and above.

items.title: CSCvw02532 - Infrastructure: Login fails when the LDAP Policy is deployed on a server running firmware version 4.1(2a) and higher
items.text: Login fails when the LDAP Policy is deployed on a server running firmware version 4.1(2a) and higher.
items.workaround: If this issue occurs, do one of the following: 1) Disable "Enable Encryption" in the LDAP policy and redeploy or, 2) Configure the other two parameters "Enable Binding Certificate" and "Download LDAP CA Certificate" on the server or, 3)Configure the secure LDAP capability introduced in 4.1(2a) directly on the server - Secure LDAP is not supported on Intersight LDAP policy.

items.title: CSCvx03878 - Infrastructure: Unable to delete M.2 Virtual Drive
items.text: M.2 Virtual Drive does not get deleted from the Storage Controllers tab on the UI or through API. This happens because the virtual drive gets marked as bootable.
items.workaround: The user needs to login to Cisco IMC and delete the virtual drive from there.

glossary.name: ACI
glossary.description: Cisco ACI is a software defined networking solution, offered by Cisco for data centers and cloud networks. Cisco ACI enables automation of networking moves, additions, and changes in the data center.

glossary.name: ACI Mode
glossary.description: Differs from NX-OS Mode in that ACI Mode is used for more complex deployments.

glossary.name: Advisories
glossary.description: Advisories alert you to potential software threats and provide recommendations for resolution.

glossary.name: Anomalies
glossary.description: An anomaly is a persisting deviation in a quantity from the expected value, for example, the systematic difference between a measurement and a trend or a model prediction.

glossary.name: Ansible
glossary.description: Ansible is an open-source software provisioning, configuration management, and application-deployment tool enabling infrastructure as code. It runs on many Unix-like systems, and can configure both Unix-like systems as well as Microsoft Windows.

glossary.name: API
glossary.description: An application programming interface (API) is a computing interface that defines interactions between multiple software intermediaries. It defines the kinds of calls or requests that can be made, how to make them, the data formats that should be used, the conventions to follow, and more.

glossary.name: APIC (Cisco)
glossary.description: Cisco Application Policy Infrastructure Controller automates multi-cloud data center network provisioning. Cisco APIC is the main architectural component of Cisco ACI. It is the unified point of automation and management for the Cisco ACI fabric, policy enforcement, and health monitoring and optimizes performance and agility.

glossary.name: APIC (Intel)
glossary.description: Advanced Programmable Interrupt Controller

glossary.name: AppD
glossary.description: AppDynamics offers application intelligence software that enables enterprises to monitor, analyze and optimize complex environments at scale. Built on one platform, the integrated suite of applications includes Application Performance Management (APM), End-User Monitoring, Infrastructure Visibility, and Business IQ.

glossary.name: AWS
glossary.description: Amazon Web Services is a subsidiary of Amazon providing on-demand cloud computing platforms and APIs to individuals, companies, and governments, on a metered pay-as-you-go basis.

glossary.name: Azure
glossary.description: Microsoft’s public cloud service.

glossary.name: B Series Server
glossary.description: Cisco UCS B-Series Blade Servers are based on Intel® Xeon® processors. They work with virtualized and non-virtualized applications to increase performance, energy efficiency, flexibility, and administrator productivity.

glossary.name: Banzai Cloud
glossary.description:
Banzai Cloud was a company that Cisco acquired in 2021. Per the press release: “On January 15, 2021 Cisco completed the acquisition of substantially all of the assets of Banzai Cloud, Zrt., a privately held company headquartered in Budapest, Hungary, that specializes in deploying cloud-native applications securely, at scale, and in production. Their suite of open source tools reduces friction in the development, deployment, monitoring and management of cloud-native applications hosted in Kubernetes (K8s) environments.

As modern cloud-native applications become more pervasive, the environments in which these applications run are becoming thinner (containers, microservices, functions), increasingly distributed and more geographically diverse. Emerging cloud infrastructure technologies give rise to unique implementation challenges as the modern cloud-native application relies on the network to provide application and API connectivity and a runtime platform for an ever-changing cloud topology. Addressing these challenges will drive developer agility and reduce friction so they can focus on writing code while spending less time managing the environments in which their applications run.

glossary.name: Bare Metal
glossary.description: The term bare metal refers to the fact that there is no operating system between the virtualization software and the hardware. The virtualization software resides on the “bare metal” or the hard disk of the hardware, where the operating system is usually installed.

glossary.name: Bare Metal hypervisor
glossary.description:
A bare metal hypervisor or a Type 1 hypervisor, is virtualization software that is installed on hardware directly. At its core, the hypervisor is the host or operating system.

It is structured to allow for the virtualization of underlying hardware components to function as if they have direct access to the hardware. The hypervisor enables a computer to separate its operating system from its core physical structure and hardware. From this position, the hypervisor can give a physical host the ability to operate many virtual units.

glossary.name: BGP
glossary.description: Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) is an Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) standard, and the most scalable of all routing protocols. Cisco supports all IETF BGP standards, as well as most or all Internet Drafts for BGP.

glossary.name: C Series server
glossary.description: UCS C-Series Rack Servers deliver unified computing in an industry-standard form factor to reduce TCO and increase agility. Each server addresses varying workload challenges through a balance of processing, memory, I/O, and internal storage resources.

glossary.name: CIDC
glossary.description: Cisco Internet Development Center

glossary.name: CIDR Block
glossary.description: Classless Inter-Domain Routing is a method for allocating IP addresses and for IP routing. The Internet Engineering Task Force introduced CIDR in 1993 to replace the previous classful network addressing architecture on the Internet. The goal was to slow the growth of routing tables on routers across the Internet, and to help slow the rapid exhaustion of IPv4 addresses.

glossary.name: CIMC
glossary.description: Cisco Integrated Management Controller

glossary.name: Cisco Tetration
glossary.description: Cisco Tetration is a hybrid-cloud workload protection platform designed to secure compute instances in both the on-premises data center and the public cloud. These compute instances could be virtual machines, bare-metal servers, or containers. It uses machine learning, behavior analysis, and algorithmic approaches to offer this holistic workload protection strategy. This approach allows customers to contain lateral movement by implementing effective micro segmentation, proactive identification of security incidents using behavior analysis, and reduction of the attack UCSN surface by identifying software-related vulnerabilities.

glossary.name: Cloud ACI
glossary.description: Cisco Cloud Application Centric Infrastructure (Cisco Cloud ACI) is a comprehensive solution that provides simplified operations, consistent policy management and visibility across multiple on-premises data centers and public clouds or hybrid cloud environments. Cisco Cloud ACI allows customers running Cisco ACI™ in their on-premises data centers to extend their Cisco ACI policies to public clouds.

glossary.name: Clusters
glossary.description: A computer network or computer cluster or clusters usually designates a number of networked computers that can be seen from the outside in many cases than a computer. In general, the individual elements of a cluster are connected over a fast network. The aim of the “clustering” is mostly in the increase in computing capacity or availability to a single computer. As are in a cluster computer (and nodes (nodes English) or servers) are often referred to as a server farm.

glossary.name: Colo
glossary.description: A colocation center (also spelled co-location, or colo) is a type of data centre where equipment, space, and bandwidth are available for rental to retail customers. Co-location facilities provide space, power, cooling, and physical security for the server, storage, and networking equipment of other firms and also connect them to a variety of telecommunications and network service providers with a minimum of cost and complexity.

glossary.name: Competitive War Room
glossary.description: Resource for competitive analyses.

glossary.name: Consul
glossary.description: HashiCorp Consul is a software first released in 2014 for DNS-based service discovery and provides distributed Key-value storage, segmentation and configuration. Registered services and nodes can be queried using a DNS interface or an HTTP interface.

glossary.name: Container
glossary.description: A container is a standard unit of software that packages up code and all its dependencies so the application runs quickly and reliably from one computing environment to another.

glossary.name: Container Orchestration
glossary.description: Container orchestration is the automation of much of the operational effort required to run containerized workloads and services. This includes a wide range of things software teams need to manage a container’s lifecycle, including provisioning, deployment, scaling (up and down), networking, load balancing and more.

glossary.name: Converged Infrastructure
glossary.description: Converged infrastructure is a way of structuring an information technology (IT) system which groups multiple components into a single optimized computing package. Components of a converged infrastructure may include servers, data storage devices, networking equipment and software for IT infrastructure management, automation and orchestration.

glossary.name: CSDL
glossary.description: Cisco Secure Development Lifecycle ensures consistent product security through proven techniques and technologies, reducing the number and severity of vulnerabilities in software. Conforms with the guidelines of ISO 27034.

glossary.name: CSR
glossary.description: CSR-100v is a Cisco server.

glossary.name: CVDs
glossary.description: Cisco Validated Designs (CVDs) provide the foundation for systems design based on common use cases or current engineering system priorities. They incorporate a broad set of technologies, features, and applications to address customer needs. Each one has been comprehensively tested and documented by Cisco engineers to ensure faster, more reliable, and fully predictable deployment.

glossary.name: Datacenter
